content_body:
Labor Day marks the end of summer and the perfect opportunity to bid farewell to the fun-filled season with a memorable getaway. Just 90 miles north of Atlanta, Blue Ridge offers an idyllic retreat that promises relaxation and adventure. Whether you're seeking a peaceful escape or an active getaway, spending Labor Day in Blue Ridge is sure to rejuvenate your spirit.

Outdoor Adventure:

Labor Day and the lake go hand in hand, and Lake Blue Ridge does not disappoint. The pristine waters of Lake Blue Ridge provide a perfect setting for boating, kayaking, paddle boarding or simply basking in the sun at Morganton Point. As the day draws to a close, consider enjoying a lakeside picnic or a peaceful sunset cruise.

For those seeking an active holiday weekend, the Blue Ridge Mountains promise an array of hiking trails and waterfall excursions. Visit Sea Creek Falls, The Swinging Bridge, or Fall Branch Falls for local treasures, and consider visiting Brasstown Bald or Amicalola Falls for stunning views located about an hour or so away.

Live Music Opportunities:

Labor Day in Blue Ridge would not be complete without the many opportunities to enjoy a craft beer, live music, and food trucks! Check out Tipping Point Brewing Co. and Grumpy Old Men Brewing Company over the weekend!

If vineyards are more up your alley, Chateau Meichtry will be hosting live music at their stunning location. 

Lastly, The Kentucky Headhunters will be performing at Tooney's Music Venue in McCaysville, GA on September 3rd! Purchase tickets online to see this CMA and Grammy Award Winning country rock band knock your socks off!

Local Events:

To experience a glimpse of the rich culture of Blue Ridge, GA, enjoy one of these events during your Labor Day getaway!

Annually held since 1981, the Labor Day BBQ and Gospel Music Show will be held over the weekend, with Monday being the biggest day. Savor the vividness of gospel music and learn about its history!

Inspired by the iconic "I Love Lucy" episode, Paradise Hills Winery hosts the beloved Grape Stomp Fest, complete with live music, BBQ, and grape stomping, of course! Join them in celebrating this 8th annual event, and make sure to purchase tickets in advance.

To wind down on your holiday weekend, visit Ott Vineyards in Ellijay for Namaste and Cabernet! Enjoy a glass of wine, a yoga class set to live music, and beautiful views.

Cabin Serenity:

For those who would rather prioritize relaxation over a 3-day weekend, reserve one of our luxury cabins to catch up on some R&R. Whether a pool table, hot tub, river access, or King suites are on your wishlist, our Blue Ridge cabins offer the perfect respite from the hustle and bustle. Reserve online or with our reservation specialists today at (706) 432-2140!
title: How to Spend a Labor Day Getaway in Blue Ridge, GA
